item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Title: Glass-Ball.jpg
Author: Tttrung
Release year: 2005

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image used in the article appears to be from here, with no indication that it has been released under a license compliant with the wiki's (CC BY-SA 3.0). Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article was posted in 2012, six years before Hollow Knight came out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this a Hollow Knight reference?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP is simple, with a slight hook (I feel as if this object would love smooth jazz), yet it needs something extra. Why do the different components need to be stored separately? Are there any theories on what the number could mean?

This article feels incomplete and the author seems to have abandoned the page (the premature sentence about the numbers would take seconds to fix). No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really know if you changed it but… it's kind of garbagey, cat. No hook, overdone concept, can't stand on its own and it isn't even memorable in any way.

Furthermore what does

It is not known at this time what this number corresponds to

refer to? You just leave it hanging there, it makes no sense.

Downvoted.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
Did you know, that some of the links in the wiki are dead, but we still see their ancient image?

The wikiwalk/crosslink timer is running out! Come join in on the fun!!!

item.title: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Roll that frog footage!
You've got some real soul, brother.

First and foremost, I think a link back to the Fifthist hub would be an idea crosslink, so we'd simply turn the already-in-place-reference into an in-line link thusly:

SCP-1724 was recovered during a raid on a facility belonging to a religious group known as the Fifthist Church

to

SCP-1724 was recovered during a raid on a facility belonging to a religious group known as the Fifthist Church

Secondly, I combed through the site for the D-class referenced here, and had a few hits. As D-class are people too, I think we could give at least one of them more context. So I propose:

In regard to a score of 567, to D-0987.

In regard to a score of 567, to D-0987.

Last but not least, we've got a direct reference to another Fifthist SCP. At first, I was going to make the referenced name itself a link, but I feel it's too easter-eggy to spoil so blatantly. So, a compromise:

From

Talk to Big Top sometime.

to

Talk to Big Top sometime.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Milo359, this comment is pretty much spam. Furthermore, staff have already told you to calm down and avoid making these kinds of posts—the giant text is unnecessary. Please don't use giant text in any further posts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your upvote levels for this comment if Wikidot would let us upvote comments…

¡¡¡¡¡¡¡¡¡THEY'RE OVER NINE THOUSAAAAAAND!!!!!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the last sentence refers to a number. No number is mentioned being displayed, prior to this, just images. The last sentence doesn't belong in this paragraph. […] One problem—you discuss at least part of what this number means in the following paragraph.

All of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What happens with the D-Class would be better (and even a bit creepier) without the "monthly termination" stuff, as this is really good otherwise. First D-Class tries to get transferred multiple times until expiring. Second D-Class "self-terminates" before another experiment can even be done with him. Third one keeps smiling… and smiling… and smiling… until dying.

Where they still smile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The D-Class's soul levels… They're over 900!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just love Roget! The Fifthist stuff never gets old. It feels like, here, the Foundation kind of screwed up. Considering Fifthists to be a hostile organization, we (Foundation) unwittingly provided them with human fodder! Brilliantly executed form of an awesome idea!

I wish I could upvote it twice, lol!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoa there friend, slow down!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love lorn. You need love. Dr. Bright near now. Go near Bright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP idea: The Lovematic Grandpa

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought I had said this when it was posted, but apparently not, so I'll mention it now.

I don't like this, and to date, it's probably the only Fifth Church thing I really dislike. It doesn't do anything new with the GoI, just rehash aspects from other SCPs, and the Fifthist stuff is all the SCP even has going for it.

It's a workable concept done in the easiest, blandest way possible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Emh… how is stated the number goes from zero to 999, if the tests were halted, and the higher one was 997? could easily be people with numbers higher than 999… and no one says the lower has to be zero.
Yeh, is a small nitpick, but really bothered me. doesn't seem logical to state the numbering unless you -know- that's how the machine works. And that can't be claimed here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The picture isn't the whole thing, there are bits off camera.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally I thought this was great - it does lean a little on prior knowledge of 1523 but I think the concept fits well into the 'southern fried Scientology' interpretation of the Fifthists. My only complaint would be that what's pictured doesn't remotely fill 34 square meters as stated :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, ultimately I'm downvoting this after I finally read the other Fifthist church pieces. Fair or not, all those are excellent and creepy, with a small hint of wacky and an enormous amount of scary or potentially scary WTF.

This… this is just hanging onto that small hint of wacky. And that's it. That's all she wrote.

Roget, I know you can push this more, and therefore you should.

edit: months later my dislike for this has only deepened :|

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
CASANOVA

HOT TAMALE

HUBBA-HUBBA

LUKE WARM LUKE

LOVELORN

COLD FISH

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I…. I like it? But I don't like it… I don't know, it seems like some sort of funky retro-futuristic (but not quite zeerust) thingamahoozits - which is good - that somehow reads your mind/soul - which is good - and it's evaluated by a real smooth soul brother that (if you're "good" enough) inspires you to try to celebritize yourself - which is good.

Somehow these three good things don't mesh well with each other.. I'm not sure if it's supposed to be explicitly part of the Fifthist church or not but, I don't know, I'm a bit iffy overall on how it all splices together.. I'm not gonna vote right now, might come back later and decide.

Edit: Upon getting home, I decided the above is certainly not enough to take away from the coolness of the thing. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ^This guy gets it. Good idea and all but it's just a frame item for more Fithist shenanigans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Take away the Fifthist and what is left? What here is interesting as it's own piece without the wacky hip celebrity cult?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, you get what I was trying to imply. This is what makes it fifthist. Not the funky lingo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You aren't reading into it man-It's a goi based on a cult of famous people. Look at what this does to your personality and mind. What kind of successful person is an attention whore? A celebrity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really believe it as a Fifthist item. I only had to read it once to figure out what was going on, and it's lacking in WTFness.

He's got a point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This thing helps you be a celebrity. Interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wouldn't mind this as its own thing, but I don't really believe it as a Fifthist item. I only had to read it once to figure out what was going on, and it's lacking in WTFness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. The whole talking part (and I really love the dialogue, I really do) and idea behind the church is excellent, but as it stands the item itself can only stay standing because of its ties to the GOI, which at this point isn't much more then what Smapti said.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A GOI needs more meat to it than "ghosts who talk like funky hep cats about people's souls".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea, but the writing needs some fine tuning.

SCP-1724's anomalous effect will activate if a person sits in SCP-1724-3 while SCP-1724's components are within 20 meters of each other. If this occurs, the different components of SCP-1724 will activate. SCP-1724-1 will begin to exude green colored smoke, and the valve will begin to turn at an accelerated pace. Note that at this point, the radiation emitted by SCP-1724-2 will not have any effect on the the person seated in SCP-1724-3. After approximately 30 minutes have passed, the television monitors located on SCP-1724-3 will begin to display images. These images usually relate in some way to the person seated in SCP-1724-3, however their context is usually impossible to determine. This will continue for an additional thirty minutes. It is not known at this time what this number corresponds to.

Images form, relating to the person seated in -3, but the last sentence refers to a number. No number is mentioned being displayed, prior to this, just images. The last sentence doesn't belong in this paragraph. In addition, the last sentence ends in a preposition, which I would not expect in a technical paper. It should read something like "It is not known at this time as to what this number means." One problem-you discuss at least part of what this number means in the following paragraph.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kind of a fad religion. Makes… Stuff that sounds like a hippy obsessed with the soul or something.

Magical pot anyone?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't hate it. But this group of interest (not even a full GOI yet) is rapidly turning into a skeleton. Make sure an SCP can stand on its own WITHOUT a group. Though this GOI does have some relation to its objects directly. So I suppose that's a moot point. Dunno. Will think on it more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's… it's alright, I guess.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah. The reaction is super fitting - this is a boring object whose only virtue is its link to a GOI and some fluff around that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We Cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are we done yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are We the Shark Punching Center Dedicated to Punching Anomalous Paintings of Sharks Held by the Fifthest Church Yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are We the Broken Hand of the Fifthist Global University Insurgency Yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are We Fifthist Church Yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Innnnnteresting. The list of reactions made this worth an upvote.

inb4 everyone hates the Fifthist Church.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eeyup.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's a fifthist? Is it a new group of interest or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah way, I saw that documentary Terminator 2, it's totally "self-terminated".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It rides with the flow, bro. Can't smell it, got no spirit, got no pull, just Big Cheese's soul-gauge tool.

That said, self-termination is something you should expunge from your dictionary. It's called "committed suicide".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Southern Baptist Scientology. Just like Momma used to make. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seems a little technological for a Fifthist item (everything else we've got from them is quirky while this is mechanical.)

Otherwise it's kind of boring, I don't really see the hook here.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Southern Baptist version of Scientology.
*just in case someone thinks I wrote it, just because I posted first, Roget did this, this was just the first thing that popped in my head after I read it.*
